frameworks.name: excitation-inhibition (E/I) balance
frameworks.one_line: Excitatory and inhibitory activity assumed balanced across spatial and temporal scales.

frameworks.name: magnetic resonance spectroscopy (edited MRS)
frameworks.one_line: In vivo measurement framework for glutamate and GABA concentrations.

core_question: Are regional glutamate and GABA concentrations correlated in the healthy resting human brain?
key_assumptions: Excitatory and inhibitory neural activity are balanced at multiple scales
MRS Glx and GABA+ levels reflect underlying neurotransmitter pools
E/I balance has a measurable neurochemical signature
manipulations: Statistical control for site, scanner vendor, and demographics
Statistical control for neurochemicals tied to neuronal density and metabolism
key_findings: Glx and GABA+ levels were highly correlated across the corpus
Correlation persisted after controlling for site, vendor, and demographics
Controlling for density/metabolism neurochemicals significantly reduced the correlation
interpretations: Findings are consistent with the notion of regional E/I balance
GABA+ and Glx levels may be critically linked to regional metabolism
